name: General Electric Employees
description_short: Gas & Electric Employees Credit Union is a state-chartered Iowa credit union serving gas and electric workers and their families since 1932, with over 400 members and personalized financial services.
description_long:
Gas & Electric Employees Credit Union (GEECU) was founded in 1932 with just 81 members and $177.80 in shares, making it one of the longest-established credit unions in Iowa. The institution was created specifically to serve gas and electric industry workers and their families, reflecting its origins as an employee-focused cooperative financial institution. Over more than 90 years, GEECU has grown from three initial loans totaling $75 into a vital community financial resource serving Mason City, Iowa.

GEECU offers comprehensive financial solutions including savings accounts, loan products, and other banking services tailored to its membership base. The credit union operates under a state charter in Iowa and maintains physical banking presence in Mason City with limited operating hours (Monday-Friday 10:30 am-1:00 pm). Members can access home banking services and apply for loans online, indicating some digital banking capability alongside traditional in-person services. The institution also conducts annual member meetings and community events, emphasizing member engagement.

What distinguishes GEECU is its hyper-local focus and 90+ year history of serving a specific occupational community. Unlike larger regional or national credit unions, GEECU maintains a small, tight-knit membership of approximately 400 people, allowing for personalized service and community-oriented decision-making. The credit union explicitly emphasizes transparency, trust, integrity, and financial education as core values, positioning itself as a member-focused partner rather than a profit-driven institution.

GEECU is best suited for current and former gas and electric utility workers in Iowa and their immediate families who value personal relationships with their financial institution and are willing to work within limited hours and a smaller product range. The credit union's small size and specialized membership base mean it is not a general-purpose bank for most consumers, and its limited hours and geographic focus create practical barriers for those outside its immediate community.
services: Loan products (specific types not detailed on website)
Community events and member engagement activities
pros: Established credit union with 90+ year history of stability and trust in the community
Member-focused cooperative model prioritizes member benefits over profit maximization
Personalized service with emphasis on transparency and financial education
State-chartered institution providing regulatory oversight and consumer protections
Online home banking and loan application services available
Member community engagement through annual meetings and social events
Low membership of 400 allows for personalized attention and local decision-making
cons: Very limited operating hours (10:30 am-1:00 pm weekdays only) restricts member access
Membership restricted to gas and electric workers and their families, excluding most consumers
Single physical location in Mason City, Iowa with no mention of branches or ATM network
No information provided about loan types, rates, fees, or specific account offerings
Small institution size limits product range compared to larger regional or national credit unions
best_for: Current and former gas and electric utility workers in Mason City, Iowa and surrounding areas
Family members of utility industry employees seeking personalized, community-based banking
Members prioritizing long-term relationships with local institutions over convenience and product breadth
Consumers comfortable with limited hours and minimal digital banking infrastructure
diagnosis: Gas & Electric Employees Credit Union is best for utility industry workers in Iowa seeking personalized, relationship-based banking from a trusted 90-year-old cooperative institution. The primary caveat is that membership is restricted to gas and electric workers and their families, and the credit union's minimal hours and single location make it impractical for most consumers outside its specific occupational community.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_description: General Electric Employees — NCUA-insured credit union in Milford, CT. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
